Agent1 Round 1: The key indicator for AFP (Acute Flaccid Paralysis) surveillance is typically based on maintaining high sensitivity to detect poliovirus. The World Health Organization recommends that countries maintain an incidence rate of at least one confirmed, non-polio AFP case per year per 100,000 children under five years old as this threshold helps ensure adequate sampling for poliovirus detection.
Answer: B
